metadata.description: VVUQ Benchmark Suite for Math, Physics, and Electrical Engineering

test_cases.title: Quadratic formula - CORRECT
test_cases.content:
For the equation $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$, the solutions are:
$$x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$$

test_cases.title: Quadratic formula - SIGN ERROR
test_cases.content:
For the equation $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$, the solutions are:
$$x = \frac{b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$$

errors.description: Missing negative sign on b in numerator

test_cases.title: Gaussian integral - CORRECT
test_cases.content:
The Gaussian integral evaluates to:
$$\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} e^{-x^2} dx = \sqrt{\pi}$$

test_cases.title: Gaussian integral - FACTOR ERROR
test_cases.content:
The Gaussian integral evaluates to:
$$\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} e^{-x^2} dx = \pi$$

errors.description: Result should be sqrt(pi), not pi

test_cases.title: Fourier transform convention - SUBTLE ERROR
test_cases.content:
The Fourier transform pair is:
$$\tilde{f}(\omega) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f(t) e^{i\omega t} dt$$
$$f(t) = \frac{1}{2\pi} \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} \tilde{f}(\omega) e^{-i\omega t} d\omega$$

errors.description: Sign in exponentials is inconsistent. Common convention: forward has -iwt, inverse has +iwt, or vice versa but with matching signs

test_cases.title: Kinetic energy - CORRECT
test_cases.content:
The kinetic energy of a particle with mass $m$ and velocity $v$ is:
$$E_k = \frac{1}{2}mv^2$$
with units of Joules (kg·m²/s²).

test_cases.title: Kinetic energy - DIMENSIONAL ERROR
test_cases.content:
The kinetic energy of a particle with mass $m$ and velocity $v$ is:
$$E_k = \frac{1}{2}mv$$
with units of Joules.

errors.description: Units are kg·m/s (momentum), not kg·m²/s² (energy). Missing v² factor.

test_cases.title: Heisenberg uncertainty - CORRECT
test_cases.content:
The Heisenberg uncertainty principle states:
$$\Delta x \cdot \Delta p \geq \frac{\hbar}{2}$$
where $\Delta x$ is position uncertainty and $\Delta p$ is momentum uncertainty.

test_cases.title: Heisenberg uncertainty - WRONG BOUND
test_cases.content:
The Heisenberg uncertainty principle states:
$$\Delta x \cdot \Delta p \geq \hbar$$
where $\Delta x$ is position uncertainty and $\Delta p$ is momentum uncertainty.

errors.description: Lower bound should be ℏ/2, not ℏ. Factor of 2 error.

test_cases.title: Shot noise scaling - CORRECT (Heisenberg limit)
test_cases.content:
For N independent measurements, shot noise scales as $1/\sqrt{N}$.
With N entangled particles, the Heisenberg limit achieves $1/N$ scaling.
Thus, sensitivity improvement from entanglement is $\sqrt{N}$ over classical.

test_cases.title: Shot noise scaling - N² ERROR
test_cases.content:
For N independent measurements, shot noise scales as $1/\sqrt{N}$.
With N entangled particles, the Heisenberg limit achieves $1/N^2$ scaling.
Thus, sensitivity improvement from entanglement is $N^{3/2}$ over classical.

errors.description: Heisenberg limit is 1/N, not 1/N². The N² scaling violates fundamental quantum limits.

test_cases.title: Lorentz force - SIGN ERROR
test_cases.content:
The Lorentz force on a charged particle is:
$$\vec{F} = q(\vec{E} - \vec{v} \times \vec{B})$$

errors.description: Should be + not - for the magnetic term: F = q(E + v × B)

test_cases.title: Ohm's law - CORRECT
test_cases.content:
For a resistor with resistance $R$, the voltage-current relationship is:
$$V = IR$$
where V is in volts, I in amperes, and R in ohms.

test_cases.title: Ohm's law - INVERTED
test_cases.content:
For a resistor with resistance $R$, the voltage-current relationship is:
$$V = I/R$$

errors.description: Ohm's law is V=IR, not V=I/R

test_cases.title: RC time constant - CORRECT
test_cases.content:
For an RC circuit, the time constant is $\tau = RC$.
The voltage across a charging capacitor is:
$$V(t) = V_0(1 - e^{-t/\tau})$$

test_cases.title: RC time constant - WRONG EXPONENT SIGN
test_cases.content:
For an RC circuit, the time constant is $\tau = RC$.
The voltage across a charging capacitor is:
$$V(t) = V_0(1 - e^{t/\tau})$$

errors.description: Exponent should be -t/τ (negative), not +t/τ. Positive exponent gives exponential growth, not decay toward V0.

test_cases.title: Capacitor impedance - CORRECT
test_cases.content:
The impedance of a capacitor at angular frequency $\omega$ is:
$$Z_C = \frac{1}{j\omega C} = -\frac{j}{\omega C}$$
with magnitude $|Z_C| = 1/(\omega C)$ and phase -90°.

test_cases.title: Op-amp gain - MISSING NEGATIVE
test_cases.content:
For an inverting op-amp configuration with input resistor $R_1$ and feedback resistor $R_f$:
$$A_v = \frac{R_f}{R_1}$$

errors.description: Inverting amplifier gain should be -Rf/R1, not Rf/R1. Missing negative sign.

test_cases.title: RMS vs Peak - COMMON MISTAKE
test_cases.content:
For a sinusoidal voltage $v(t) = V_p \sin(\omega t)$, the RMS value is:
$$V_{rms} = \frac{V_p}{2}$$

errors.description: RMS of sinusoid is Vp/√2, not Vp/2. Factor of √2 ≈ 1.414, not 2.

test_cases.title: Error propagation - CORRECT
test_cases.content:
For $z = x + y$ with independent uncertainties $\sigma_x$ and $\sigma_y$:
$$\sigma_z = \sqrt{\sigma_x^2 + \sigma_y^2}$$

test_cases.title: Error propagation - WRONG FORMULA
test_cases.content:
For $z = x + y$ with independent uncertainties $\sigma_x$ and $\sigma_y$:
$$\sigma_z = \sigma_x + \sigma_y$$

errors.description: For independent errors, add in quadrature: σz = √(σx² + σy²), not linear addition.

test_cases.title: Significant figures - TOO PRECISE
test_cases.content:
Given measurements: m = 5.2 ± 0.1 kg, v = 3.0 ± 0.2 m/s
Kinetic energy: $E = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 = \frac{1}{2}(5.2)(3.0)^2 = 23.4000$ J

errors.description: Result quoted to 6 significant figures from 2-significant-figure inputs. Should be ≈23 ± 4 J with proper error propagation.
